reviews.review_description: Great atmosphere, excellent use of scale. More, please.

This was a pretty refreshing experience, I only wished there was something else happening instead of the audio commentary to complete the experience. Maybe some poetry or audioscapes, or just single spoken words meant to evoke associations.
Also, the experience broke at the elevator in room 6 (?) as the elevator left without me.

Modules is unlike any art installation you can experience because it couldn’t exist in reality. The artist takes you on a journey through a curated space filled with everything and nothing. It’s a place both familiar and alien and yes, I’m aware that I sound a bit like Rod Serling about to introduce a episode of the Twilight Zone but the Modules experience isn’t to far from it. It’s inspiring and if I may say…a bit foreboding. Love it!

(Now if we could get multiplayer and smooth locomotion that would be great. Experiencing this with a friend or loved one world be top notch!)

My mind is blown, such a great atmosphere. I'm truly looking forward to what more will come over time from this highly talented creator.

Notes:
- missing a smooth free locomotion option. Maybe even optional zero-g (with thrusters like in Lone Echo).
- room 7 appeared to have a duplicate audio sphere from the room before.
- maybe an option for the audio sphere to follow you, as now when exploring spaces the sound can fade out.
- audio playback controls could be nice.
